conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Exenatide requires dose adjustment based on kidney function and is not recommended in severe chronic kidney disease due to reduced clearance and potential accumulation
contraindications.riskDescription: Potential drug accumulation and increased risk of adverse effects in severe kidney impairment
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other GLP-1 receptor agonists with better tolerability in CKD such as liraglutide or dulaglutide
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Exenatide: 10 μg twice daily, increased to 20 μg twice daily; CKD adjustment: Use with CrCl >30 ml/min, not recommended with CrCl <30 ml/min

conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 45 ml/min per 1.73 m² or less
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Extended-release formulation of exenatide has limited data for use in moderate to severe chronic kidney disease and requires careful monitoring
contraindications.riskDescription: Limited safety and efficacy data in patients with moderate to severe kidney impairment
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other GLP-1 receptor agonists with better evidence in CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Exenatide extended-release: 2 mg once weekly; CKD adjustment: Use with eGFR >45 ml/min per 1.73 m², limited data for eGFR 15-45
